summary: When the NestJS application starts, the bootstrap function in src/main.ts initializes the framework using the AppModule. This triggers the Dependency Injection container to instantiate the defined controllers and services before opening the network port to listen for incoming requests.

nodes.title: Entry Point
nodes.explanation: The bootstrap function is invoked to create the application instance via NestFactory.

nodes.title: Module Initialization
nodes.explanation: The AppModule is processed to resolve metadata, specifically registering the AppController and AppService.

nodes.title: Dependency Injection
nodes.explanation: The AppService is instantiated as a provider, ready for injection into other classes.

nodes.title: Controller Setup
nodes.explanation: The AppController is instantiated with the injected AppService, mapping its routes to the NestJS runtime.

nodes.title: Server Listening
nodes.explanation: Once the dependency graph is resolved, the application begins listening on the configured port (3000).
